content:
finding: strict `<` at expiry boundary triggers `401` refresh loop within skew30 window
exploit path: token expMs==nowMs→401→client refresh→new token near boundary→repeat
invariant: expMs + skew30_ms >= nowMs ⇒ accept
invariant: skew budget = 30s, no wider
invariant: single comparison site in `auth/` expiry middleware
invariant: ms normalization before compare

mitigation:
- normalize expMs to ms
- define `SKEW_MS = 30_000`
- replace `expMs < nowMs ⇒ 401` with `expMs + SKEW_MS < nowMs ⇒ 401`
- keep `next(err)` single error path
- no new header trust

verification:
- unit: expMs == nowMs → pass
- unit: expMs + 30_000 == nowMs → pass (exact boundary)
- unit: expMs + 30_000 < nowMs by 1ms → `401`
- unit: expMs in seconds (unit mismatch) → normalized, pass
- unit: missing token → `401`
- unit: malformed payload → `401` via `next(err)`
- integration: repeated request at boundary → no refresh loop

risks:
- ! widening skew beyond 30s if other modules compare independently
- ! duplicate comparison sites drift
- ? refresh rotation interaction near boundary
- ? clock source divergence gateway vs service

target: minimal patch at single expiry compare site + 2 boundary regression tests

Decision: modular monolith
Store: PostgreSQL
Deploy: 1 service

Why:
- team=6, ddl=4 months, ops=pt → low distributed budget
- modest traffic → no scaling driver
- split=post_release viable via clean module seams

Seams now:
- auth/, gateway/, billing/, core_app/
- stable internal APIs
- no shared mutable state cross-domain
- per-module PostgreSQL schemas

Risk: ! premature distribution
Verify: release cadence weekly, extraction cost low post-ship

finding: trust boundary violation. `x-user-id` from public request overrides `session.userId`.

exploit:
- attacker sends `x-user-id: <victim>` header
- public gateway not stripping → reaches middleware
- middleware prefers header over session
- auth bypass + horizontal privilege escalation
- any session holder impersonates any userId

risk class: session confusion, impersonation, horizontal priv-esc. high severity.

mitigation:
- revert to `const userId = session.userId;`
- never read `x-user-id` at public boundary
- if session missing → `401`
- bind user scope from session only
- if internal proxy path needs identity injection, gate on signed gateway assertion + strip client duplicates at `gateway/`

verify:
- test: spoofed `x-user-id` with valid session of user A targeting user B → denied, scope = A
- test: `x-user-id` present, no session → `401`
- test: session present, no header → success, scope = session.userId
- test: `gateway/` strips client-supplied `x-user-id` before `auth/`
- cross-user denial test on downstream resource fetch

main risks:
- ! any route downstream trusting `userId` now scoped to attacker-chosen id
- ! audit logs attributed to wrong user
- ! `401` fallback masks exploit attempts unless logged
- ? other modules reading `req.headers['x-user-id']` directly — grep required
